title: Baseline anchored late-interaction residual scorer

queries: ColBERT late interaction MaxSim token-level retrieval candidate scoring
residual learning preserve baseline scorer bounded residual ranking

papers.title: ColBERTv2: Effective and Efficient Retrieval via Lightweight Late Interaction
papers.claim: Late interaction keeps token-granular representations and scores candidates with lightweight token-level matching instead of collapsing each side to one vector.
papers.relevance: This motivates using prompt-token to candidate-label-token interaction as a bounded residual over the raw-label baseline score surface, preserving token-level evidence without per-candidate autoregressive rollout.

papers.title: PLAID: An Efficient Engine for Late Interaction Retrieval
papers.claim: Late-interaction retrieval can be made practical with tensorized and optimized scoring rather than recursive candidate decoding.
papers.relevance: This supports the throughput gate for a Stage 3 scorer that remains candidate-aware but avoids the shared-prefix long-tail bottleneck.

evidence.why_now: EXP099 and EXP108 remain the strongest evidence that explicit candidate-token signal matters, but their exhaustive or shared-prefix forms are not acceptable final paths. EXP159 showed that preserving the raw-label baseline score surface with a bounded pairwise residual softened but did not solve the salt-side collapse. EXP168 showed naive late interaction improves over the repeated 0.678576 plateau but is still far below baseline. EXP171/174 further weakened shared-prefix and Poly-encoder follow-ups. The next justified diagnostic is therefore not a residual-scale scan: keep the raw-label baseline scorer as the main surface and add only a centered, tanh-bounded late-interaction token residual.

hypothesis: If naive late interaction loses too much of the already-useful raw-label baseline ordering, then treating late interaction as a small centered residual rather than a replacement/additive main scorer should preserve the salt-side baseline surface while still exposing token-level sample-candidate evidence. The interface remains finetune-only, candidate-aware, non-autoregressive, and Stage4-compatible because it uses one prompt encoding plus batched candidate-token matching rather than target-database-only fixed heads or O(L*C) autoregressive rollout.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This is not an autocomplete_residual_scale scan: use the existing fixed residual scale only to test the information path.

This candidate is different from EXP159 because the residual is token-level late interaction over selected prompt tokens and class-id/raw-label candidate tokens, not another pairwise pooled-label feature residual.
This candidate is different from EXP168 because the raw-label baseline score surface remains the main decision surface and the late-interaction contribution is centered and tanh-bounded.
Do not add autocomplete_rank_auxiliary, change autocomplete_poly_codes, scan token budget, or alter prompt/sampling/basis settings in this bundle.
Before launch, run py_compile, git diff --check, JSON validation, and a remote item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity check.
Throughput gate: do not launch EXP177/178/179 if item-incoterms shows a long-tail signature comparable to the old shared-prefix roughly 1.25 train steps/sec path.
Exclude GPU0 for this candidate only because recent EXP162 evidence showed a pre-eval SIGTERM when user-churn used GPU0 as local_rank 0; global pipeline_config keeps gpu_exclude empty.
Completed and finalized on 2026-05-11 with global verdict: failed.
Final metrics: EXP177 user-churn roc_auc=0.684794629214976; EXP178 user-ltv mae=78.94613023620798; EXP179 item-incoterms mrr=0.7044836795691288.
Result under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse.

Throughput evidence: EXP179 item-incoterms ran on four ranks with per-rank batch 1 at roughly 7.4 train steps/sec after warmup, i.e. about 29.6 effective train items/sec; final test progress was around 16.38 it/sec. This is not a practical-throughput failure.

Interpretation: baseline-anchored late interaction did not preserve the salt-side score surface. It stayed above the repeated 0.678576 collapse band but below EXP168 late interaction and far below the strict baseline mrr=0.8147880873466811, so this is a fixed-screen scientific failure rather than a retune-plausible signal.

Search-space consequence: do not hand-scan autocomplete_residual_scale, late-interaction token budget, autocomplete_poly_codes, ranking margin, or auxiliary weight. Move to a different paper- or ablation-backed Stage4-compatible candidate-aware information path.
